question_text: Two forces act on a box on a frictionless surface: Force A is 8 N to the east, and Force B is 6 N to the north. A third force C is then applied. After Force C is applied, the resultant force on the box is zero (the box is in equilibrium). Calculate the magnitude of Force C and explain which direction it must act.
correct_answer: Force C must have a magnitude of 10 N and act at an angle of approximately 217° (or southwest, at 37° south of west). This is calculated using Pythagoras' theorem: √(8² + 6²) = √(64 + 36) = √100 = 10 N. Force C must be opposite and equal to the resultant of Forces A and B.
explanation: For equilibrium, all forces must cancel out, meaning the resultant must be zero. Forces A and B combine to create a resultant of 10 N to the northeast (using Pythagoras). Force C must be equal in magnitude but opposite in direction to balance this, so it acts at 10 N to the southwest. This is an example of Newton's First Law where zero net force means no acceleration.
